summary: Deprecated audit trail for GraphicsDrivers overlay-plane overrides. The current app writes HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers\DisableOverlays = 1, but this research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source that publishes DisableOverlays as a supported registry control.

setting.name: Graphics Overlay Planes
setting.casual_explanation: Windows and the graphics driver stack can use overlay planes to present some content more directly. People usually touch this only when they are troubleshooting display flicker, black screens, browser artifacts, or similar graphics bugs.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable overlay planes when they suspect a driver or display path problem tied to overlays and want a fallback composition path.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning overlays off can help some display troubleshooting scenarios, but it can also change presentation behavior and performance characteristics. Because this research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source for DisableOverlays, the current app write stays blocked from validated publication.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft publishes several GraphicsDrivers debug keys for Timeout Detection and Recovery under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers. The current app writes DisableOverlays = 1 on that same path. This record treats DisableOverlays as an observed app implementation only, because the current research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source that documents DisableOverlays and its exact supported semantics.

allowed_values.label: Normal graphics baseline
allowed_values.meaning: No explicit overlay-plane override is published by this record.

allowed_values.label: Observed app overlay disable override
allowed_values.meaning: The current app treats DisableOverlays = 1 as an overlay-plane disable setting, but this record has not validated that mapping from a primary Microsoft source.

targets.notes: Tracked as an observed registry override only.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableOverlays = 1 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers, but this research pass has not yet captured a primary Microsoft source that publishes the value as an official overlay-plane control.

windows_defaults.label: Conservative baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems where this dataset has not yet validated DisableOverlays as an official Microsoft control surface

states.rationale: Do not publish a supported graphics override until a primary Microsoft source is captured.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline is conservative and keeps the unvalidated graphics override out of published presets.

recommended_profiles.label: Hold for research

states.rationale: Do not publish or apply the override until the exact DisableOverlays mapping is sourced.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to keep the area documented without overstating confidence.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a primary-source Microsoft-backed overlay-plane registry control.
tradeoffs: The app behavior is documented honestly.
The override stays blocked from validated publication.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting what the app currently writes for overlay planes.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a validated Microsoft registry mapping.
tradeoffs: The current app behavior is captured.
The exact overlay-plane control semantics are still unvalidated.
recommended_profiles.notes: Blocked until a primary Microsoft source for DisableOverlays is captured.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: TDR Registry Keys
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents supported GraphicsDrivers debug keys such as TdrDelay, TdrDdiDelay, TdrLevel, TdrLimitCount, and TdrLimitTime under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers. This research pass used that page as the nearest official GraphicsDrivers override surface, but did not find DisableOverlays published there.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers\DisableOverlays = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The app targets a real graphics troubleshooting area, but this research pass has not yet captured a primary Microsoft source for DisableOverlays and its exact semantics.
blocking_issues: No primary Microsoft source for DisableOverlays was captured in this research pass.
The current app write cannot be published as a validated overlay-plane control until its value semantics are sourced.

open_questions: Is DisableOverlays documented in a Microsoft primary source outside the current GraphicsDrivers debug pages?
Should the app present overlay-plane changes as experimental graphics troubleshooting actions rather than validated tweaks?

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Microsoft Learn: TDR Registry Keys: Microsoft documents supported GraphicsDrivers debug keys such as TdrDelay, TdrDdiDelay, TdrLevel, TdrLimitCount, and TdrLimitTime under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers. This research pass used that page as the nearest official GraphicsDrivers override surface, but did not find DisableOverlays published there.
